Saturday was perfect for a long hike up the mountain trail behind the lake. The weather was clear and cool, with autumn leaves turning orange along the ridge. I packed sandwiches and a thermos of coffee for the summit. After getting home I spent the evening cooking a slow-braised lamb stew with rosemary and root vegetables, letting it simmer for three hours while I read the new novel I picked up at the bookstore. The story follows a lighthouse keeper in the Scottish Highlands during the nineteen forties. Sunday morning I slept in, made pancakes, and sat on the porch watching the birds. It was the most relaxing weekend I have had in months, exactly the kind of break I needed before the busy week ahead.
